codeReview.summary: Reviewed the original failed feature diff (c33ab81f7a101eebf13ea2528f4a268d4218fdf8) and the fix diff (08beeb4e7d2f80af4395505be33e8867b463b08d). The fix addresses the prior blocking gap by carrying directory-scoped REVIEW.md instructions as per-path trusted data, resolving the nearest review text for each changed file during context assembly, threading those per-change instructions into downstream ReviewRequest payloads, and preserving strict ai-review.yaml warning surfacing through loader warnings with targeted regression coverage.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker completed a rules/context threading fix under the go-integration skill, but the work and tests were primarily backend/rules/context logic that more closely matches go-backend's stated scope.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: features.json lists beta-repo-config-layer-correctness-fix with skillName=go-integration, while the implementation changed internal/rules/loader.go, internal/context/assembler.go, and related unit tests; .factory/skills/go-backend/SKILL.md explicitly covers 'rules loading and config precedence' plus 'diff/context assembly'.

summary: Pass. I reviewed the fix transcript skeleton plus both commits. The original commit still had a single run-wide REVIEW winner and dropped ai-review warnings; the fix commit corrects that by storing directory reviews per path, using ReviewForPath during assembly, passing changed paths into rules.Load from the processor, and proving end-to-end per-change request threading along with surfaced YAML warnings.
